name: Hypercorn (ASGI/HTTP2/HTTP3 Server)

performance.notes: HTTP/2 and HTTP/3 support; competitive with uvicorn

agent_readiness.notes: ASGI server with HTTP/2 and HTTP/3 (QUIC) support. hypercorn myapp:app --bind 0.0.0.0:8000. asyncio and trio event loops. WebSocket support. Server-Sent Events. Graceful shutdown. TLS: --certfile/--keyfile. HTTP/2: supported by default. HTTP/3: requires aioquic. Config: --config config.toml. Workers: --workers 4. Alternative to uvicorn when HTTP/2 or HTTP/3 needed. Quart (async Flask) creator's ASGI server.

gotchas: Less popular than uvicorn — smaller ecosystem and fewer examples
Graceful restart less smooth than nginx + uvicorn
Not as well supported by Gunicorn-like process managers
best_when: Trio event loop preference over asyncio
avoid_when: Standard FastAPI/Starlette deployment (uvicorn simpler and more battle-tested)
summary: Hypercorn is an ASGI server supporting HTTP/1.1, HTTP/2, and experimental HTTP/3 (QUIC). hypercorn app:app --bind 0.0.0.0:8000. asyncio and trio event loops. WebSocket and SSE. TLS built-in. Alternative to uvicorn when HTTP/2 is required. Created by Philip Jones (also created Quart). Less commonly used than uvicorn but better for HTTP/2-required deployments.
